title: NOC 40020 – Administrators – Post-Secondary Education and Vocational Training | GTR Immigration
description: Administrators – Post-Secondary Education and Vocational Training under NOC 40020 are senior leaders responsible for managing colleges, universities, trade schools, and vocational institutions across Canada. Classified under TEER 0 (Management Occupation), this role represents a high-skilled pathway within Canada’s education sector. With Canada continuing to expand international student intake, workforce retraining programs, and vocational education initiatives, qualified education administrators remain in steady demand nationwide.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group40 – Managers in public administration, education and social and community servicesSub-Major Group400 – Managers in public administration and educationMinor Group4002 – Managers in educationNOC Code40020

duties: Plan, organize, direct, and evaluate academic activities
Direct registration systems and academic records
Oversee course scheduling and graduation requirements
Manage operations of private colleges or institutes
employment_requirements: Faculty administrators: Graduate degree related to the academic faculty and several years of experience as a university professor or college instructor
Registrars: Undergraduate degree in business administration or related field plus experience in registration administration
Vocational training administrators: Undergraduate degree in business administration or subject expertise and certification
Leadership experience and senior-level academic management are typically required.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $58,200 to $132,200, depending on institution type and province.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 40020 remains stable, supported by:
Expansion of vocational and technical training
Institutions with strong industry partnerships and international collaborations may offer enhanced career opportunities.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 40020 are employed in:
Strong opportunities exist in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Quebec, Saskatchewan, and Manitoba.
Strong public or institutional pension benefits
Leadership roles in Canada’s education system
Professionals with international academic leadership experience may find attractive opportunities, especially in institutions with global student populations.
